item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-4453 regards its containment as a “vacation” or “sabbatical” away from its own family

My upvote was yours to lose at this point.
The fantastic writing of the rest just reinforced it; paints a fantastic rounded portrait of a figure that hasn't had nearly enough exploration in modern media. Incident 42/C had me in stitches.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That was an ambitious article and some really good characterization, although a bit more novelty would be appreciated. I'm surprised that this article is less successful than how it should be.

Also to the author, would you mind if I translate this into Vietnamese? The Vietnamese SCP Wikidot site is not yet official, so I would like to post it both on that site and on my personal blog. I will send you the links once you have given me a thumbs up and the translation process is completed. Thanks in advance!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Omg this was just awesome, the amount of detail to give Greek gods a life in the present and the intricacy of the relationships being told so smoothly, and the open ending, I loved every little bit of it and the work you put on it is noticeable! If you ever put up a Tale telling the ending to this SCP PLEASE do let me know, your storytelling is just amazing!
+1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I absolutely adore this. Bonus points for the sweet-ass references to Dionysus' older origin stories!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: shit goddammit motherfuck that is so much better shit fuckdammit

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have never seen this much effort and detail put into characterizing Hera, and it is fascinating to see. This was extremely well done.

PS: Really feeling like the anti-zeus MTF should be called the Shock-Blockers :).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I disagree immensely with the notion that the goddess of the nuclear family is going to suddenly change the values she's had for oh…2 millennia but otherwise +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
i now have a new favorite SCP.
I look forward to seeing more of your work
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ugh, that really gripped me.
Amazing work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: lol I though about writing a tale like that. Zeus being served the papers and then deciding he wants to keep Hera. But that'll be a while away.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: this is top stuff, now all they need is to send some to chat to H and apologise on Hera's behalf and try and talk him round.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is quite the good article. I need some assurance that you're totally going to write the divorce process, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wouldn't make sense for a goddess to go against her own nature, but I still like the ending. It was a good twist to end a good SCP article.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That was beautiful, a real treat to read.

+1

item.title: Here's 4453
item.content:encoded:
Hey! Glad to have this one out finally, been sitting on it for a few months now.

Thanks to everyone who helped crit it, your advice was invaluable. Kybard, Timo 8474, Silentwatcher, CB and anyone else from the chat who helped out, you guys were amazing.
